"Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image, neither rear you up a standing image, neither shall ye set up any image of stone in your land, to bow down unto it: for I am the LORD your God." The witnesses note the foundational command against idols — the crux of the worship of God alone. On "Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image... neither shall ye set up any image of stone in your land, to bow down unto it: for I am the LORD your God," interpreters expound the ground of the covenant. The reformed Calvin and the dominant reading note the prohibition of idols — "Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image": the chapter of blessing and curse opens with the foundational command against all idolatry; the witnesses note the significance — the prohibition of every form of idol (the molten idol, the graven image, the standing pillar, the figured stone), none to be made or worshipped; the lesson — the worship of God alone, the first and second commandments restated, the foundation of the covenant, "thou shalt have no other gods before me... thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image... thou shalt not bow down thyself to them," Exodus 20:3-5; the for I am the LORD your God (the ground, the covenant relation demanding exclusive worship); the figure — the exclusive worship of the true God as the foundation of all blessing, the rejection of every idol the first duty of the covenant people, "little children, keep yourselves from idols," 1 John 5:21; the worship in spirit and truth that admits no image, John 4:24; the foundation on which the blessings and curses that follow are built.
